data.title: GERDAU S.A. reports 2Q25 Adjusted EBITDA of R$2.6 billion, up 7% from 1Q25, with North America operations contributing 61%.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, São Paulo reported a notable achievement with an Adjusted EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) of R$2.6 billion, showing a 7% increase from the first quarter of the same year. The operations in North America played a significant role, representing 61% of the total performance.

data.title: Bunge Global SA finalizes $1.3 billion senior notes offering.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the United States has dropped to 4.2%, which is the lowest it has been in the past 20 years. The economy added a total of 531,000 jobs in the month of October, surpassing expectations. The decrease in unemployment and the increase in job numbers signal a positive trend for the country's economic recovery. This development is a result of various factors, including the reopening of businesses, increased vaccination rates, and government stimulus efforts. Despite this progress, there are still concerns about inflation and ongoing challenges in certain sectors, such as supply chain disruptions. Overall, the decrease in unemployment and job growth indicate a significant step forward in the nation's economic recovery post-pandemic.

data.title: Data breach at Genoa Community Hospital, NE, may have exposed personal/health info. Residents urged to monitor accounts.
data.description: In Genoa, Nebraska on July 31, 2025, Genoa Community Hospital/LTC, also known as Genoa Medical Facilities, has disclosed a potential data security breach impacting personal and/or protected health information. Genoa is prioritizing the protection of privacy and security for all individuals affected by this incident.

data.title: CLEAR partners with White House and CMS to create secure digital health ecosystem, supporting CMS's initiative with IAL2 identity layer for major healthcare partners.
data.description: CLEAR is playing a crucial role as the IAL2 identity layer for key healthcare partners in the industry. With a focus on supporting the nationwide initiative by CMS to modernize care through trusted and interoperable technologies, CLEAR is driving significant advancements. This initiative is aimed at enhancing healthcare services on a national scale, enabled by the strong support and collaboration of major healthcare partners. By serving as the IAL2 identity layer, CLEAR is contributing to the progress in healthcare modernization and paving the way for improved patient care experiences.

data.title: Amazon's retail demand surpasses expectations with increased spending, cloud division sees tighter margins. Future uncertain.
data.description: The e-commerce giant's website saw higher consumer spending than anticipated. Additionally, margins narrowed within the company's crucial cloud computing sector.

data.title: CareerBuilder + Monster successfully closed sale transactions with BOLD, Iron Corp U.S. Inc., and PartnerOne, maximizing value for stakeholders. Concludes Chapter 11 process.
data.description: CareerBuilder + Monster, a company that recently concluded its court-supervised sale process under Chapter 11 in Chicago on July 31, 2025. The transactions yielded significant value for stakeholders while ensuring continuity for clients and employees. This marks a pivotal moment for the Company as it finalized the sale process with successful outcomes. The details surrounding the transactions and values have not been disclosed.

data.title: CMS launches Outcome-Based Care Blueprint to improve Medicare, reduce costs. TEAM model begins Jan 1, 2026 at 742 hospitals, targeting $19.2 billion in expenses.
data.description: Today, a final rule was issued aimed at enhancing health outcomes and cutting costs for millions of Americans receiving the five most expensive medical procedures. The new Transforming Episode Accountability Model (TEAM) is set to be implemented on January 1, 2026, at 742 hospitals across the nation. Its goal is to reduce the $19.2 billion currently spent on these procedures.

data.title: Louisiana Fish Fry names Joe Tuza as new CEO after 3 decades of experience.
data.description: The appointment of Joe Tuza as Chief Executive Officer has been announced by Louisiana Fish Fry ("LFF"), a top manufacturer and distributor of authentic Louisiana-inspired food products. With over three decades of experience, Tuza will lead the company in Baton Rouge, La., starting July 31, 2025.

data.title: Researcher proposes a ‘Lean’ network for Ethereum to boost speed, resist quantum threats, and simplify the layer-1 blockchain.
data.description: The proposal seeks to enhance Ethereum's speed and make it quantum-resistant, as well as to simplify the technical complexities of the layer-1 blockchain.
